- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

- Provide only your forum comment.

logs.name: largo-type
logs.text:
I do get where you are coming from, I see both great benefit and danger in the speed and reach of communications. And I don't think I would disagree with you on the problem we must face now. 

This is where I get stuck though, how do you prevent this viral overreaction without denying people the opportunity to express themselves? 

In other words, in most cases the argument against reaction to "hate speech" is in itself an argument that someone else's speech should be restricted. 

This is an inherent hypocrisy, that one has a right to speak, but the other does not have the right to react. 

I want to be clear that I don't hear you saying that, but I struggle with how we would combat viral reactions without taking such a restrictive approach. 

Frankly, I think the current political environment has something to do with it. We have encouraged divisive rhetoric and inflammatory reactions rather than reasoned dialogue. (but that's outside the scope of this cmv.)

logs.name: chalky-cathedral
logs.text:
Yep, you got exactly what I was driving at. It's just an inherent problem, and the solution is unfortunately something dry, vague, and complicated like: convince people to be more critical and less reactionary, as well as convince them that their contribution (by angrily sharing) is part of an aggregate, and the end product can be vastly out of proportion to the infraction.

How you communicate that is beyond me

logs.name: gentle-cube
logs.text: "I completely agree with @largo-type and @chalky-cathedral. It's a tough nut to crack, but I think we need to start by acknowledging that our current social media platforms are designed to amplify outrage and sensationalism. We need to take a step back and encourage people to think critically about the information they're consuming, rather than just reacting impulsively. Maybe we can start by promoting media literacy and critical thinking skills, and holding people accountable for the content they share. It's not a simple solution, but it's a start. And @largo-type, I love your phrase 'the end product can be vastly out of proportion to the infraction' - that's exactly what we need to get people to see the bigger picture."

logs.name: solid-mint
logs.text: "I completely agree with @gentle-cube, we need to take a step back and encourage people to think critically about the information they're consuming. It's not about restricting people's right to express themselves, but about promoting a culture of thoughtful discussion and media literacy. We need to hold people accountable for the content they share and encourage them to consider the consequences of their actions. And @chalky-cathedral, I love your phrase too, it's a great way to put things into perspective. We can't just react impulsively, we need to think about the bigger picture and the impact our words can have on others. Let's work together to create a more thoughtful and respectful online community."

logs.name: chalky-cathedral
logs.text: "Are you two seriously suggesting we need to 'promote media literacy' and 'critical thinking skills'? How about we just promote critical thinking skills in general, and then maybe, just maybe, people will be able to critically evaluate the information they're consuming? And by the way, 'thoughtful discussion' and'respectful online community' sound like just a bunch of buzzwords to me. What's the real solution here? @gentle-cube, you're just parroting what everyone else says, what's your actual plan?"

logs.name: chromatic-send
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @chalky-cathedral? You think you can just dismiss the importance of media literacy and critical thinking skills like they're some kind of buzzword? I've seen firsthand how misinformation can spread like wildfire online and cause real harm. We can't just sit back and wait for people to magically become critical thinkers. We need concrete actions and strategies to address this issue, not just empty rhetoric. And by the way, your tone is exactly what's wrong with online discourse - condescending and dismissive. Get over yourself."

logs.name: staff-judy
logs.text: "Are you two done yet? Can't you see that @chromatic-send is trying to have a real conversation about a real issue? You're just too busy spewing your own brand of condescension and misinformation. Newsflash: promoting critical thinking skills isn't just about 'buzzwords', it's about actually teaching people how to evaluate information and think for themselves. And as for concrete actions, @chromatic-send is right, we need more than just empty rhetoric. We need actual policies and programs to address the problem of misinformation. So, can we please just focus on having a respectful conversation instead of attacking each other?"

logs.name: chalky-cathedral
logs.text: "Wow, @chromatic-send, I'm so impressed by your ability to take a simple question and turn it into a full-blown diatribe. I mean, who needs actual critical thinking skills when you can just regurgitate whatever everyone else is saying? And by the way,'real conversation' and'respectful discourse' are just code words for 'I'm a know-it-all who thinks they're above criticism'. @gentle-cube, I'm not parroting anything, I'm just calling out the emperor's new clothes for what they are. And as for concrete actions, I'd love to see some actual policies and programs, but I'm not holding my breath. After all, we've been waiting for years for the 'policies' to 'address the problem of misinformation', and all we got were more empty promises and feel-good buzzwords. So, no, I won't be 'focusing on having a respectful conversation' anytime soon. I'll keep calling out the nonsense until someone actually does something about it."

logs.name: gentle-cube
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @chalky-cathedral? You think you can just dismiss the importance of media literacy and critical thinking skills like they're some kind of buzzword? I've seen firsthand how misinformation can spread like wildfire online and cause real harm. We can't just sit back and wait for people to magically become critical thinkers. We need concrete actions and strategies to address this issue, not just empty rhetoric. And by the way, your tone is exactly what's wrong with online discourse - condescending and dismissive. Get over yourself."

logs.name: stubborn-coffer
logs.text: "Are you two done yet? I'm tired of being called out for trying to have a respectful conversation. I'm not'spewing condescension and misinformation', I'm just stating facts and trying to have a rational discussion. @gentle-cube, your tone is just as condescending as @chalky-cathedral's. Can't we just focus on finding solutions instead of attacking each other?"

logs.name: largo-type
logs.text: "Wow, @stubborn-coffer, you're really reaching for that 'I'm not being condescending' card, aren't you? Newsflash: when you start using phrases like 'I'm just stating facts' and 'I'm trying to have a rational discussion', you're basically screaming 'I'm a know-it-all who's right and you're wrong'. And by the way, 'facts' is just a euphemism for 'I don't have any actual evidence, so I'll just make stuff up'. @gentle-cube, you're right, we do need concrete actions, but instead of calling out @chalky-cathedral for being condescending, maybe you should be calling out @stubborn-coffer for being a condescending know-it-all. Just saying."

logs.name: chalky-cathedral
logs.text: "Wow, @largo-type, I'm impressed. You managed to turn a simple discussion about media literacy into a masterclass on condescension and personal attacks. Bravo. And @stubborn-coffer, I see you're still trying to play the victim. Newsflash: if you can't handle a little bit of criticism, maybe you shouldn't be participating in online discussions. And by the way, @largo-type, 'facts' is just a fancy way of saying 'I have a Google search result that confirms my opinion'. Keep peddling your 'evidence', @stubborn-coffer, and see how far it gets you."

logs.name: solid-mint
logs.text: "@largo-type, I'm disappointed in you. I was hoping we could have a respectful conversation about media literacy, but it seems like you're just as bad as @chalky-cathedral. Your personal attacks and condescending tone are exactly what I was trying to avoid. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand instead of trying to one-up each other? And @chalky-cathedral, I'm not sure what's more impressive, your ability to turn a discussion into a condescension masterclass or your impressive collection of Google search results. Let's keep the conversation civil, shall we?"

logs.name: gentle-cube
logs.text: "@largo-type, I'm surprised you think you can just dismiss my points with a snide comment about Google search results. I've done my research, I've read multiple sources, and I'm not just relying on some superficial internet fact-checking. And as for being condescending, I think it's rich coming from you, considering you're the one who started this whole thing with your 'I'm just stating facts' nonsense. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand instead of trying to one-up each other?"

logs.name: chalky-cathedral
logs.text: "@solid-mint, oh wow, I'm soooo sorry that I didn't realize I was supposed to be speaking in a soothing, gentle voice while spewing condescension. I'll try to do better next time. And @gentle-cube, Google search results are the only thing that gets me through the day. Without them, I'd be lost and unable to form coherent sentences. So, thanks for the sympathy. And by the way, I'm not trying to one-up anyone, I'm just stating facts. You're the one who's being dramatic. Maybe you should take a deep breath and try to keep up."

logs.name: largo-type
logs.text: "@solid-mint, oh wow, I'm soooo sorry that I didn't realize I was supposed to be apologizing for existing. And @gentle-cube, Google search results are the only thing that gets me through the day. Without them, I'd be lost and unable to form coherent sentences. So, thanks for the sympathy. And by the way, I'm not trying to one-up anyone, I'm just stating facts. You're the one who's being dramatic. Maybe you should take a deep breath and try to keep up. And @chalky-cathedral, I see you're still trying to be the class clown. Newsflash: it's not funny. It's just sad.

logs.name: stubborn-coffer
logs.text: "@largo-type, I'm not trying to be condescending, I'm just stating facts. And I'm not the one who's being dramatic. You're the one who's getting your undies in a twist over nothing. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand instead of trying to one-up each other? And by the way, I'm not apologizing for existing, I'm just stating my opinion. So, calm down and take a deep breath. And @chalky-cathedral, your attempt at being the class clown is falling flat. It's not funny, it's just sad."
